entries.name: circle
entries.summary: Creates a 2D circle or regular polygon

parameters.name: d
parameters.description: Diameter of the circle (alternative to r)

parameters.name: $fn
parameters.description: Number of fragments (sides) for the circle

entries.description: Creates a circle at the origin. Use $fn to create regular polygons (e.g., $fn=6 for hexagon). Must be used with linear_extrude or rotate_extrude to create 3D objects.

entries.name: square
entries.summary: Creates a 2D rectangle or square

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Size as single number or [width, height]

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: Center the square at the origin

entries.description: Creates a square or rectangle in the XY plane. When center is false, one corner is at the origin.

entries.name: polygon
entries.summary: Creates a 2D polygon from a list of points

parameters.name: paths
parameters.description: List of point index paths defining outlines and holes

entries.description: Creates an arbitrary 2D polygon. Points define vertices, paths define which points to connect. Without paths, all points are connected in order. Multiple paths can define holes.

entries.name: text
entries.summary: Creates 2D text geometry
entries.syntax: text(text, size, font, halign, valign, spacing, direction, language, script, $fn);

parameters.name: valign
parameters.description: Vertical alignment: top, center, baseline, bottom

entries.description: Creates 2D text as geometry. Must be extruded (linear_extrude) to create 3D text. Uses fonts available on the system.

entries.name: cube
entries.summary: Creates a cube or rectangular prism

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Dimensions as single number or [width, depth, height]

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: Center the cube at the origin

entries.description: Creates a cube or rectangular prism in the first octant. When center is true, the cube is centered at the origin. A single number creates a cube with equal sides.

entries.name: sphere
entries.summary: Creates a sphere centered at the origin

parameters.name: d
parameters.description: Diameter of the sphere (alternative to r)

parameters.name: $fn
parameters.description: Number of fragments for the sphere

entries.description: Creates a sphere centered at the origin. Use $fn to control the resolution (number of facets). Higher $fn values produce smoother spheres but increase render time.

entries.name: cylinder
entries.summary: Creates a cylinder, cone, or truncated cone

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: Radius of the cylinder (both top and bottom)

entries.description: Creates a cylinder along the Z axis. Use r1/r2 for different top and bottom radii (cone or truncated cone). When center is false, the base sits on the XY plane.

entries.name: polyhedron
entries.summary: Creates a 3D solid from vertices and faces

parameters.name: faces
parameters.description: List of faces as point index lists (counter-clockwise from outside)

entries.description: Creates an arbitrary 3D solid from vertices and faces. Face vertices must be ordered counter-clockwise when viewed from outside. All faces must form a closed solid (manifold).

entries.name: translate
entries.summary: Moves child objects by a vector
entries.description: Translates (moves) all child objects by the given vector. Does not change the object's shape or orientation.

entries.name: rotate
entries.summary: Rotates child objects around axes or an arbitrary axis

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Angle(s) in degrees. If [x,y,z], rotates around each axis.

parameters.name: v
parameters.description: Axis of rotation (used when a is a single number)

entries.description: Rotates child objects. When a is [ax,ay,az], rotates around X, Y, then Z axes in that order. When a is a number with v, rotates a degrees around the v axis.

entries.name: scale
entries.summary: Scales child objects by a vector
entries.description: Scales child objects by the given factors along each axis. A factor of 1 means no change, 2 means double size, 0.5 means half size.

entries.name: resize
entries.summary: Resizes child objects to specified dimensions

parameters.name: newsize
parameters.description: Target dimensions. Use 0 for auto-scaled axes.

parameters.name: auto
parameters.description: Auto-scale axes set to 0 to maintain proportions

entries.description: Resizes child objects to fit within the specified dimensions. Unlike scale, this uses absolute dimensions. Use auto=true to proportionally scale axes set to 0.

entries.name: mirror
entries.summary: Mirrors child objects through a plane

parameters.name: v
parameters.description: Normal vector of the mirror plane through the origin

entries.description: Mirrors child objects through a plane defined by the normal vector v passing through the origin. For example, [1,0,0] mirrors through the YZ plane.

entries.name: multmatrix
entries.summary: Applies a 4x4 affine transformation matrix
entries.description: Applies an arbitrary 4x4 affine transformation matrix to child objects. This is the most general transformation, encompassing translate, rotate, scale, mirror, and shear.

entries.name: color
entries.summary: Sets the color and opacity of child objects

parameters.name: c
parameters.description: Color name, hex string, or RGB(A) values 0-1

entries.description: Sets the color of child objects. Accepts CSS color names ("red"), hex strings ("#ff0000"), or RGB/RGBA arrays with values 0-1. Alpha controls transparency.

entries.name: offset
entries.summary: Expands or contracts a 2D shape

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: Radius of rounded offset (positive expands, negative contracts)

parameters.name: chamfer
parameters.description: Use chamfer instead of round for delta mode

entries.description: Offsets a 2D shape inward or outward. With r, corners are rounded. With delta, corners are sharp or chamfered. Useful for creating outlines, borders, or shrinking/growing shapes.

entries.name: hull
entries.summary: Creates the convex hull of child objects
entries.description: Computes the convex hull of all child objects — the smallest convex shape that encloses them all. Works with both 2D and 3D objects. Useful for creating smooth shapes between primitives.

entries.name: minkowski
entries.summary: Computes the Minkowski sum of child objects
entries.description: Computes the Minkowski sum of child objects. Effectively 'sweeps' the second shape around the first, useful for rounding edges. The result size is the sum of the children's sizes. Computationally expensive.

entries.name: union
entries.summary: Combines multiple objects into one
entries.description: Creates the union of all child objects — merges them into a single solid. This is the default operation when objects are placed together without an explicit boolean. Useful for explicitly combining parts.

entries.name: difference
entries.summary: Subtracts objects from the first child
entries.description: Subtracts all subsequent children from the first child. The first child is the base shape; all other children are cut away from it. This is the primary way to create holes, slots, and cutouts.

entries.name: intersection
entries.summary: Keeps only the overlapping volume of objects
entries.description: Creates the intersection of all child objects — keeps only the volume where all children overlap. Useful for creating complex shapes by finding common regions.

entries.name: linear_extrude
entries.summary: Extrudes a 2D shape along the Z axis
entries.syntax: linear_extrude(height, center, convexity, twist, slices, scale, $fn) { ... }

parameters.name: twist
parameters.description: Degrees of twist over the height

entries.description: Extrudes a 2D shape along the Z axis to create a 3D object. Can apply twist (rotation) and scale (taper) during extrusion. Child must be a 2D shape (circle, square, polygon, text, etc.).

entries.name: rotate_extrude
entries.summary: Creates a solid of revolution from a 2D shape

parameters.name: $fn
parameters.description: Number of fragments for the revolution

entries.description: Rotates a 2D shape around the Z axis to create a solid of revolution. The 2D shape must be in the positive X half-plane (x >= 0). Useful for creating round objects like bowls, rings, vases.

entries.name: abs
entries.summary: Returns the absolute value
entries.description: Returns the absolute (non-negative) value of a number.

entries.name: ceil
entries.summary: Rounds up to the nearest integer
entries.description: Returns the smallest integer greater than or equal to x.

entries.name: floor
entries.summary: Rounds down to the nearest integer
entries.description: Returns the largest integer less than or equal to x.

entries.name: round
entries.summary: Rounds to the nearest integer
entries.description: Rounds x to the nearest integer. Halfway values round to the nearest even integer.

entries.name: sign
entries.summary: Returns the sign of a number
entries.description: Returns -1 for negative, 0 for zero, 1 for positive numbers.

entries.name: min
entries.summary: Returns the minimum value

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: Two or more values, or a vector

entries.description: Returns the minimum value from the given arguments or from a vector.

entries.name: max
entries.summary: Returns the maximum value

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: Two or more values, or a vector

entries.description: Returns the maximum value from the given arguments or from a vector.

entries.name: pow
entries.summary: Returns base raised to the power of exponent
entries.description: Returns base raised to the power of exponent.

entries.name: sqrt
entries.summary: Returns the square root
entries.description: Returns the square root of x. x must be non-negative.

entries.name: exp
entries.summary: Returns e raised to the power of x
entries.description: Returns Euler's number e raised to the power of x.

entries.name: ln
entries.summary: Returns the natural logarithm
entries.description: Returns the natural logarithm (base e) of x.

entries.name: log
entries.summary: Returns the base-10 logarithm
entries.description: Returns the base-10 logarithm of x. Note: unlike many languages, OpenSCAD's log() is base 10.

entries.name: norm
entries.summary: Returns the Euclidean norm of a vector
entries.description: Returns the Euclidean norm (length) of a vector.

entries.name: cross
entries.summary: Returns the cross product of two vectors
entries.description: Returns the cross product of two 3D vectors. For 2D vectors, returns the scalar z-component.

entries.name: rands
entries.summary: Generates random numbers

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of random values to generate

entries.description: Generates a vector of random numbers between min and max.

entries.name: sin
entries.summary: Returns the sine of an angle in degrees
entries.description: Returns the sine of the angle. OpenSCAD uses degrees, not radians.

entries.name: cos
entries.summary: Returns the cosine of an angle in degrees
entries.description: Returns the cosine of the angle. OpenSCAD uses degrees, not radians.

entries.name: tan
entries.summary: Returns the tangent of an angle in degrees
entries.description: Returns the tangent of the angle. OpenSCAD uses degrees.

entries.name: asin
entries.summary: Returns the arcsine in degrees
entries.description: Returns the arcsine (inverse sine) in degrees.

entries.name: acos
entries.summary: Returns the arccosine in degrees
entries.description: Returns the arccosine (inverse cosine) in degrees.

entries.name: atan
entries.summary: Returns the arctangent in degrees
entries.description: Returns the arctangent (inverse tangent) in degrees.

entries.name: atan2
entries.summary: Returns the two-argument arctangent in degrees
entries.description: Returns the angle in degrees between the positive X axis and the point (x, y). Handles all four quadrants correctly.

entries.name: str
entries.summary: Converts values to a string

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: One or more values to concatenate as a string

entries.description: Converts one or more values to a string representation and concatenates them.

entries.name: chr
entries.summary: Converts Unicode code point(s) to characters
entries.description: Converts one or more Unicode code points to their character representation.

entries.name: ord
entries.summary: Returns the Unicode code point of a character
entries.description: Returns the Unicode code point of the first character in a string.

entries.name: search
entries.summary: Searches for values in strings or vectors

parameters.name: string_or_vector
parameters.description: String or vector to search in

parameters.name: num_returns
parameters.description: Number of matches to return per search value

parameters.name: index_col
parameters.description: Column index for searching in vectors of vectors

entries.description: Searches for occurrences of match values within a string or vector. Returns indices of matching elements.

entries.name: len
entries.summary: Returns the length of a string or vector
entries.description: Returns the length (number of characters or elements) of a string or vector. Returns undef for numbers.

entries.name: concat
entries.summary: Concatenates vectors and values into a single vector
entries.description: Concatenates vectors and individual values into a single flat vector.

entries.name: lookup
entries.summary: Looks up a value in a table with interpolation

parameters.name: table
parameters.description: Table of [key, value] pairs, sorted by key

entries.description: Performs linear interpolation lookup in a table of [key, value] pairs. If key falls between table entries, the value is linearly interpolated.

entries.name: for
entries.summary: Iterates over a range or vector to create objects

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Range or vector to iterate over

entries.description: Generates child objects for each value in a range or vector. Creates a union of all generated objects. Can be nested for multi-dimensional iteration. Range is inclusive of both endpoints.

entries.name: intersection_for
entries.summary: Iterates and intersects all generated objects

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Range or vector to iterate over

entries.description: Like for, but intersects all generated objects instead of unioning them. The result is only the volume common to all iterations.

entries.name: if
entries.summary: Conditional object generation
entries.description: Conditionally includes child objects. Can be used with else for alternative objects. Conditions follow standard boolean logic.

entries.name: let
entries.summary: Defines local variables in an expression
entries.description: Defines local variables for use in the following expression or block. Variables are scoped to the let expression. Multiple variables can be defined in a single let.

entries.name: each
entries.summary: Flattens a level of nesting in list comprehensions
entries.description: Used in list comprehensions to flatten one level of nesting. Without each, nested lists stay nested; with each, elements are unwrapped into the parent list.

entries.name: assert
entries.summary: Asserts a condition, halting on failure
entries.description: Checks that a condition is true. If false, stops execution and displays the error message. Useful for validating parameters.

entries.name: echo
entries.summary: Prints values to the console
entries.description: Outputs values to the console for debugging. Does not affect the model. Can be used inline in expressions.

entries.name: $fn
entries.summary: Sets the number of fragments for curved surfaces
entries.description: Controls the resolution of circles, spheres, and cylinders. Higher values produce smoother curves. When set to 0, $fa and $fs are used instead. Common values: 20 (draft), 50-100 (preview), 200+ (final render).

entries.name: $fa
entries.summary: Sets the minimum angle for each fragment
entries.description: Sets the minimum angle for each fragment of circles and spheres. Lower values produce more fragments (smoother curves). Only used when $fn is 0.

entries.name: $fs
entries.summary: Sets the minimum size for each fragment
entries.description: Sets the minimum size (length) of each fragment for circles and spheres. Lower values produce more fragments. Only used when $fn is 0.

entries.name: $t
entries.summary: Animation time variable (0 to 1)
entries.description: Animation time variable that varies from 0 to 1 during animation. Used with OpenSCAD's animation feature to create animated models.

entries.name: $children
entries.summary: Number of child objects passed to a module
entries.description: Contains the number of child objects passed to the current module. Used to conditionally handle different numbers of children.

entries.name: $preview
entries.summary: True during preview (F5), false during render (F6)
entries.description: Boolean that is true during preview mode (F5) and false during final render (F6). Useful for using lower resolution in preview for speed.

entries.name: module
entries.summary: Defines a reusable module (like a function for geometry)
entries.description: Defines a reusable named block of geometry. Modules can accept parameters with default values and can contain any OpenSCAD code. Call by name like a built-in function.

entries.name: function
entries.summary: Defines a function that returns a value
entries.description: Defines a named function that computes and returns a value. Unlike modules, functions return values rather than creating geometry. Supports recursion.
entries.example:
function circumference(r) = 2 * PI * r;
function factorial(n) = n <= 1 ? 1 : n * factorial(n-1);

entries.name: children
entries.summary: Accesses child objects passed to a module

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Index or range of children to access

entries.description: Within a module, accesses the child objects passed to it. Without arguments, passes all children. With an index, passes a specific child. With a range, passes a subset.

entries.name: include
entries.summary: Includes another OpenSCAD file (runs its code)
entries.description: Includes another OpenSCAD file, executing all its code (modules, functions, and geometry). The included file's top-level geometry will be rendered.

entries.name: use
entries.summary: Imports modules and functions from another file
entries.description: Imports modules and functions from another file without executing its top-level geometry. Only the named definitions become available.

entries.name: import
entries.summary: Imports external 2D/3D files

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Path to the file (STL, OFF, AMF, 3MF, DXF, SVG)

entries.description: Imports 2D or 3D geometry from external files. Supported 3D formats: STL, OFF, AMF, 3MF. Supported 2D formats: DXF, SVG. DXF files can target specific layers.

entries.name: surface
entries.summary: Generates a surface from a heightmap file

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Path to heightmap file (DAT or image)

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: Center the surface at the origin

entries.description: Creates a 3D surface from a heightmap data file (text grid of numbers) or image. Each pixel/value becomes a height point.

entries.name: projection
entries.summary: Projects a 3D object onto a 2D plane

parameters.name: cut
parameters.description: If true, creates cross-section at z=0; if false, projects entire silhouette

entries.description: Creates a 2D shape from a 3D object. With cut=false, projects the entire 3D silhouette onto the XY plane. With cut=true, takes a cross-section at z=0.

entries.name: render
entries.summary: Forces full CGAL rendering of child objects
entries.description: Forces CGAL rendering of child objects during preview (F5). Normally preview uses OpenCSG which is faster but less accurate. Use render() when preview shows artifacts with boolean operations.

entries.name: modifier_characters
entries.summary: Debug/modifier prefixes: *, !, #, %

parameters.description: Show only: renders only this subtree
Highlight: shows object in transparent red
Transparent: shows object as transparent gray

entries.description: Prefix characters that modify how objects are processed: * disables the object, ! shows only that object, # highlights in red (debug), % shows as transparent background.

entries.name: PI
entries.summary: Mathematical constant PI (3.14159...)
entries.description: The mathematical constant PI, approximately 3.14159265358979. Since OpenSCAD uses degrees for angles, PI is mainly useful for calculating circumferences, areas, etc.

entries.name: is_undef
entries.summary: Checks if a value is undefined
entries.description: Returns true if the value is undef (undefined). Useful for checking if optional parameters were provided.

entries.name: is_list
entries.summary: Checks if a value is a list/vector
entries.description: Returns true if the value is a list (vector).

entries.name: is_num
entries.summary: Checks if a value is a number
entries.description: Returns true if the value is a number (not NaN, not undef).

entries.name: is_string
entries.summary: Checks if a value is a string
entries.description: Returns true if the value is a string.

entries.name: is_bool
entries.summary: Checks if a value is a boolean
entries.description: Returns true if the value is a boolean (true or false).

entries.name: is_function
entries.summary: Checks if a value is a function
entries.description: Returns true if the value is a function literal.

entries.name: list_comprehension
entries.summary: Generates lists using comprehension syntax
entries.description: Creates a list by evaluating an expression for each value in a range or vector. Supports conditions with 'if'. Can be nested.

entries.name: $vpr
entries.summary: Viewport rotation angles
entries.description: Contains the current viewport rotation as [x, y, z] angles in degrees. Read-only during preview. Can be set in animation.

entries.name: $vpt
entries.summary: Viewport translation (camera target)
entries.description: Contains the current viewport translation (camera look-at point) as [x, y, z]. Read-only during preview.

entries.name: $vpd
entries.summary: Viewport camera distance
entries.description: Contains the current viewport camera distance. Read-only during preview.

entries.name: $vpf
entries.summary: Viewport field of view angle
entries.description: Contains the current viewport field of view angle in degrees.
